meta.anchor: Year 0 AC = The Collapse of the Tower of Babel · ~2200 BCE
meta.externalReference: BCE/CE dates are approximate and provided for cross-reference only. The peoples of Telumë do not use them.
meta.description: A chronicle of major events in Telumë from the Collapse of the Tower of Babel to the present age. The Haldorian Reckoning counts from Year 0 AC. Compiled from Sūrya, Haldorian, Ealdor, and Kichigamswak sources. Disputed events are noted. Gaps are preserved.
meta.lastUpdated: Year 3450 AC · ~1250 CE · Little Ice Age onset

eras.name: Before the Collapse
eras.description: The age of one language. Adzmyst spoken by all. The Rabbit walks the earth. Nokomis tends the fire. The Tower is built on the plain of Shinar.

events.event: Mategwas carries fire from the underworld. Nokomis names him Fyrsthara — the Fire-Carrier.
events.source: The Lost Tales of Telumë, Tale I

events.event: Mategwas weaves his brother Lox from twigs, grass, fur, and light. Lox squanders the gift of fire and freezes. Raccoon finds him in the spring. He learns nothing.
events.source: The Lost Tales of Telumë, Tale VII
events.disputeNote: The Sūrya priests reject this tale. They claim Lox was not woven but born, and that he froze not from carelessness but from challenging the Sun-King's ancestor.

events.event: The Tower is built on the plain of Shinar. Mategwas speaks the first mantras at its base. The builders do not listen.
events.source: The Lost Tales of Telumë · The Dawnstone · The Book of Babel

eras.name: The Collapse of the Tower of Babel
eras.description: The Tower falls. The one language shatters into five fragments. The storm god Tarḫunt departs. The Flood reshapes the Bay of Ealdforn. The peoples scatter. This is the anchor of the Haldorian Reckoning.

events.event: THE COLLAPSE OF THE TOWER OF BABEL. The one language shatters. The peoples are scattered across the face of the earth. The storm god Tarḫunt departs — where he went is not recorded. The Haldorian Reckoning begins.
events.source: Nešatta Codex, Tablets 1–2 · The Book of Babel · Genesis 11:1-9

events.event: Mategwas vanishes at the scattering. He is not seen again — except when he is.
events.source: The Lost Tales of Telumë, Coda
events.disputeNote: The Sūrya priests claim he ascended into the Sun. The Kichigamswak elders say he went north and is still running. The Nešatta archivists file him under 'whereabouts unknown — presumed present.'

events.event: The Flood. Melting ice reshapes the Bay of Ealdforn. Sea routes open. The coastline takes its present form.
events.source: Kichigamswak oral tradition · Prūsisk amber records

events.event: First siege of Aristopol by Ealdor. The Markfolk attempt to take the cliff-city at the river narrows. The siege fails. The walls hold.
events.source: Ealdenbóc, The Genealogies

eras.name: The Early Kingdoms
eras.description: The four realms take shape. Sūrya builds its temple-complex. Ealdor elects its kings. The Haldorian city-states war and trade. The Dawnstone is carved.

events.event: The Dawnstone is carved at Sūryapura. The Trilingual Codex is begun. Vedic Sanskrit, Avestan, and Old Persian on three faces of a single monolith.
events.source: The Dawnstone

events.event: THE ĀTRAVAN SCHISM. The berserker Ātarš-Gaēθa leads a night raid on three Metoullin scriptoriums. Eleven priests killed. Palm-leaf copies of the Agnihotra Śāstra burned. He paints his taunt on the wall: 'Fire is first. The king is ash.'
events.source: Sūrya court records · Ātravan oral tradition · Tihomir chronicle
events.disputeNote: Metoullin sources claim the archives were rebuilt within a year. Ātravan sources claim the fire spread to three villages. The Tihomir chronicle notes both claims and adds: 'The fire knows what it burned.'

events.event: A young Ātravan berserker — unnamed, the Fire-Place carrier — crosses into Ealdor carrying a copper brazier with a coal from the eternal flame. Peasant Cerdic, son of Wulf, encounters him on the road. King Æthelwulf places the boy with the Galdr-Gemyndigan at the Ring. The coal is still burning.
events.source: Ealdenbóc, The Genealogies · Galdr-Gemyndigan monastery records

events.event: Ātarš-Gaēθa captured and executed by fire at Sūryapura. The Sun-King lights the pyre. His last word: 'ātarš.' His sword never recovered.
events.source: Sūrya court records · Ātravan oral tradition · Tihomir chronicle
events.disputeNote: The Ātravan say the sword was smuggled to Ealdor. The Metoullin say it was melted down. The Tihomir chronicle notes: 'The young berserker at the Ring has still not spoken. The coal is still burning. The fire knows where the sword is.'

events.event: Fourth siege of Aristopol. The city nearly falls. Tsarnigrad sends ships. The siege breaks. The Haldorian city-states realize they must unify or perish.
events.source: Ealdenbóc, The Genealogies · Haldorian Republic charter preamble

events.event: THE PANKUS ACCORD. The Haldorian Republic is chartered at Nešatta. Seven city-states. No king. No capital. The assembly meets every seven years. The 'no king' clause is carved above every meeting hall.
events.source: Nešatta Codex · Pankus Accord charter

eras.name: The Long Silence
eras.description: Two and a half millennia of consolidation, oral tradition, and fragmentary records. The Agnihotra Śāstra is composed. The Ātravan go underground. The coal burns quietly at the Ring. Few written documents survive.

events.event: The Agnihotra Śāstra composed by the Metoullin priesthood. Censored by the Sun-King. The Ātravan reject it and compose the oral Ātarš Apstāna.
events.source: Agnihotra Śāstra colophon · Ātravan oral tradition

events.event: The Galdr-Gemyndigan begin oral chronicles at the Ring. The Ātravan coal is still burning. The silent brothers tend it.
events.source: Galdr-Gemyndigan oral tradition · later Ring Chronicle

events.event: The Nešatta archive reaches its present form. Glyph archive sealed in the deepest chamber.
events.source: Nešatta archive inventory

eras.name: The Age of Chronicles
eras.description: The great written compilations. Monasteries chartered. Oral traditions set on vellum. The Medieval Warm Period enables manuscript exchange.

events.event: Tihomir Monastery chartered on the Magdalen Islands. OCS monks. Witnesses, not voters.
events.source: Tihomir monastery charter

events.event: Bogumil transcribes the Nešatta Codex at Tihomir. Seven tablets. Three translatable. One missing. One unreadable. One sealed. One blank.
events.source: Nešatta Codex, Colophon

events.event: The Galdr-Gemyndigan order formally chartered at the Ring. The founding document acknowledges the silent brother and the coal. Both flames to be kept burning in perpetuity — never to touch.
events.source: Galdr-Gemyndigan charter

events.event: Brother Eadwine compiles The Lost Tales of Telumë at Tihomir. He synthesizes Sūrya, Nešatta, and Kichigamswak sources. 'They do not agree. I have not made them agree. The Rabbit does not want them to agree.'
events.source: The Lost Tales of Telumë, Coda

events.event: The Ealdenbóc set down in writing by the Galdr-Gemyndigan. Oral traditions recorded. Genealogies compiled. Marginal corrections from living skalds included.
events.source: Ealdenbóc

eras.name: The Warm Period Peak
eras.description: Medieval Warm Period at its height. Sea routes open. Trade flourishing. Pankus meets without interruption.

events.event: The Pankus meets for the 412th time. All seven city-states attend. The 'no king' clause reaffirmed. Tsarnigrad abstains.
events.source: Haldorian Republic assembly records

events.event: The Sun-King's library at Sūryapura reaches its greatest extent. Over three thousand palm-leaf manuscripts.
events.source: Sūryapura temple inventory

eras.name: The Cooling
eras.description: The Medieval Warm Period declines. The Little Ice Age begins. Sea routes narrow. The northern passages freeze. PRESENT DAY.

events.event: First recorded failure of the Pankus to convene. Sea routes impassable. Assembly postponed. Meets two years late.
events.source: Haldorian Republic assembly records

events.event: Senator Claudius writes to the Pankus quoting the Nešatta Codex. Warns of 'the cold that comes from the north and the hunger that comes from the cold.'
events.source: Haldorian Republic senatorial archives

events.event: Tsarnigrad stockpiles grain. Other city-states accuse it of hoarding. Tsarnigrad replies it is preparing for 'the winter that will not end.'
events.source: Haldorian Republic assembly records

events.event: Sea ice reaches the northern fjords of Ealdor. Fellfolk ships cannot reach open water. The Thing is deadlocked.
events.source: Ealdenbóc marginalia · Fellfolk oral reports

events.event: PRESENT DAY. The Little Ice Age advances. The Pankus convenes at Nešatta — perhaps for the last time before the ice closes the Gulf. The Nešatta Codex is read aloud. The seventh tablet is still blank. The storm god has not returned. The coal at the Ring is still burning.
events.source: Current · This chronicle is being written now

ongoing.name: The Ātravan Coal at the Ring
ongoing.description: Brought by the young berserker in 279 AC. Still burning as of 3450 AC — over three thousand years. The Galdr-Gemyndigan tend it alongside their own fire. The two flames have never touched. The current silent brother is the eighty-seventh of the line. He has never spoken. The coal is still burning.

ongoing.name: The Sword of Ātarš-Gaēθa
ongoing.description: Never recovered after his execution in 281 AC. Seventeen claims of discovery recorded. None verified. The Ātravan say it will ignite only for the true heir.
ongoing.lastRecorded: 3400 AC · ~1200 CE — Senator Claudius notes a fraudulent mercenary in Aristopol

ongoing.name: The Seventh Tablet of the Nešatta Codex
ongoing.description: Clay fired. Surface prepared. Nothing written. The storm god has not returned. Displayed at every Pankus assembly. Delegates are invited to write on it. No one has.
ongoing.lastRecorded: 3450 AC · ~1250 CE — Present day. Still blank.

disputed.description: What happened to Mategwas at the Collapse? Sūrya: ascended into the Sun. Kichigamswak: went north, still running. Nešatta: whereabouts unknown — presumed present.
Was Lox woven or born? The Sūrya priests reject the Kichigamswak weaving story. The elders reject the Sūrya birth story.
Where is the sword of Ātarš-Gaēθa? Seventeen claims. None verified.
Can a coal burn for three thousand years? The Galdr-Gemyndigan reply: the coal is not burning — the fire is. The coal is just what the fire lives in.
Is the silent brother lineage truly unbroken? Gaps in the early centuries. The current brother cannot be asked.
